review_scope: Structural and architectural dimensions of 100+ item unresolved-issues backlog (Layers A-U) to support BA scoping of implementation work

app_understanding.note: This is a workflow/harness architecture review, not a web application. No browser testing applicable. All analysis is git + filesystem + code review.

live_testing.note: No web app. Runtime analysis substituted with git status, gitignore checks, filesystem verification, and code review of scripts/score-update.sh.

console_errors_checked.note: No web app. Substituted with hook/script error path analysis.

network_requests_analyzed.note: N/A — no web app. Filesystem + git evidence substituted.

runtime_metrics_collected.note: git status --porcelain (111 entries, 25 untracked), git check-ignore on 5 paths, wc -c on tmp-cleanup.sh (12077 bytes), script line analysis

security_patterns_checked.note: Verified: agent-scores.json in HEAD tree (pending staged deletion), tool-policy.v1.json reviewed, no credentials found in reviewed files

performance_benchmarked.note: N/A — not a web application. Filesystem I/O patterns reviewed instead.

runtime_findings.note: Untracked files include docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md (the spec itself), cycle artifacts from d1e94e/75463e-DH/085647-d1722b, and tests/generated/ directory

runtime_findings.measured: git check-ignore -v docs/reference/tmp-cleanup-convention.md -> .gitignore:66:docs/*; git check-ignore -v logs/ -> .gitignore:46:logs/; git check-ignore -v agent-scores.json -> .gitignore:84:/agent-scores.json
runtime_findings.note: Three paths relevant to spec findings confirmed gitignored

runtime_findings.measured: git ls-tree HEAD -- agent-scores.json: blob 41c5227... agent-scores.json (IN HEAD). git diff --cached shows deletion staged. gitignore rule at line 84 excludes future instances.
runtime_findings.note: File is in HEAD tree, staged for deletion in index, gitignored from future commits. J7/Q6 claim of 'tracked' is in-flight, not yet shipped.

runtime_findings.measured: scripts/score-update.sh:109 max(0, min(100, score)); scripts/score-update.sh:152 new_score = max(0, min(100, old_score + delta)); agent-scores.json shows 4 ceiling events for 'dev' agent with deltas [15, 15, 6, 15] all lost
runtime_findings.note: 51 total positive delta points lost to ceiling=100 for dev agent alone

runtime_findings.measured: ls -l /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh: EXISTS, 12077 bytes. ls scripts/install/: NO_DIR (not present in repo)
runtime_findings.note: Script active on host, no in-repo mirror path exists

runtime_findings.measured: git show --stat 34210cc: 68 files changed, 3273 insertions(+), 78 deletions(-). Commit message: 'chore(orphan): unattributed session changes from prior uncommitted work'. task-id: orphan.
runtime_findings.note: Multi-subsystem mega-commit spanning agents/, commands/, hooks/, scripts/, cache/ prefixes

issues.title: docs/reference/ entirely gitignored, stranding delivered artifacts
issues.description: The .gitignore rule 'docs/*' at line 66 with exception '!docs/dev/' causes all content under docs/reference/ to be excluded from git. The file docs/reference/tmp-cleanup-convention.md (the L3 deliverable from cycle 161035) exists on disk (verified: 844 bytes, May 19 19:40) but is gitignored. git ls-files for the entire docs/reference/ directory returns empty — fresh clones receive nothing. Additionally, 11 other files under docs/reference/ (checkpoint-mechanism.md, configuration-summary.md, server-infra.md, etc.) are all silently excluded. The gitignore rule has no documented rationale (Q4): why docs/reference/ is excluded while docs/dev/ is permitted is not recorded anywhere.
issues.measured: git check-ignore -v docs/reference/tmp-cleanup-convention.md -> .gitignore:66:docs/* (GITIGNORED). git ls-files docs/reference/ -> empty (0 files tracked). 12 files exist under docs/reference/ on disk.
issues.expected: Deliverables produced by dev cycles ship via git and are available to fresh clones
issues.runtime_evidence: git check-ignore -v docs/reference/tmp-cleanup-convention.md confirmed gitignored at .gitignore:66
issues.observation_notes: Items D3, Q4. git check-ignore verified. docs/reference/ contains 12 files (combined ~42KB) all invisible to git. docs/dev/ path is NOT ignored (confirmed: git check-ignore returns NOT_IGNORED for docs/dev/).

issues.title: Spec file on tmpfs and currently untracked — pre-commit loss window
issues.description: docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md (the spec file capturing this entire backlog) exists on /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude which is tmpfs. The file is NOT gitignored (docs/dev/ is explicitly exempted), but it is currently untracked (git status shows '?? docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md'). A server reboot, umount, or OOM event before the spec is committed would permanently lose it. The refs/checkpoints/master auto-commit mechanism is the only current backstop, and it operates on a polling basis — the commit must happen before a crash.
issues.measured: git status: '?? docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md' (untracked). git check-ignore -v docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md -> NOT_IGNORED. File at /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md (tmpfs mount).
issues.expected: Spec file committed to git within one cycle of creation
issues.runtime_evidence: git status shows untracked (??). Filesystem path confirms /dev/shm location (tmpfs).
issues.observation_notes: Items E5, U3. The spec IS committable (path is not gitignored). Risk is the uncommitted window on tmpfs. The refs/checkpoints auto-commit (checkpoint runs visible in git log --all) provides partial protection.

issues.title: Entire working repo on tmpfs with 111 dirty working-tree entries
issues.description: The working repository at /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude is on tmpfs. Server reboot or umount wipes all workspace state. git status --porcelain shows 111 total entries (25 untracked, 86 modified/deleted staged/unstaged), representing multiple dev cycles worth of uncommitted work. If the host restarts before these are committed and pushed, all untracked files are permanently lost and staged changes revert to HEAD. The refs/checkpoints/master mechanism (visible as checkpoint commits in git log --all) provides partial protection for tracked files but does not capture untracked files.
issues.measured: git status --porcelain: 111 total entries. 25 entries begin with '??'  (untracked). Filesystem: /dev/shm is tmpfs (kernel ramdisk, wiped on reboot). Checkpoint commits visible: 9b383ca, 131d384, baae4ad, etc. (git log --all).
issues.expected: Working state durable across server restarts; untracked files included in checkpoint
issues.runtime_evidence: git status --porcelain count: 111 entries. git log --all shows checkpoint ref commits fire frequently but cover only tracked files.
issues.observation_notes: Items P1, P2, E10. The 25 untracked files include cycle artifacts from d1e94e/75463e-DH/085647-d1722b plus the spec itself. Refs/checkpoints/master commits are visible in git log at regular intervals (9b383ca: 2026-05-21T08:57:27, 131d384: 08:56:22, etc.) confirming the mechanism is active.

issues.title: agent-scores.json in HEAD tree with staged deletion and gitignore — in-flight J7 state
issues.description: agent-scores.json is tracked in HEAD (git ls-tree HEAD confirms blob 41c5227). A deletion is currently staged in the index (git diff --cached shows 'deleted file mode 100644'). A root-anchored gitignore rule at .gitignore:84 ('/agent-scores.json') excludes future worktree instances. The J7/Q6 claim that the file 'is tracked by git' was accurate at HEAD but the staged deletion has not yet been committed. Until the commit lands, the file remains in origin/master's tree, cluttering clones' history. The 3 historical commits touching it (4d9f9f5, 6d28883, 34210cc) remain in origin/master regardless of the staged deletion.
issues.measured: git ls-tree HEAD -- agent-scores.json: blob 41c5227 (IN HEAD). git diff --cached: 'deleted file mode 100644'. git log --oneline -- agent-scores.json: 3 commits. .gitignore:84: /agent-scores.json (root-anchored, gitignored).
issues.expected: agent-scores.json deletion committed; file excluded from future history
issues.runtime_evidence: git ls-tree HEAD confirms file in current HEAD tree. git diff --cached confirms staged deletion exists but is uncommitted.
issues.observation_notes: Items J7, Q6. The J7 spec claim is accurate for HEAD state but in-flight; remediation (gitignore rule) is already present at .gitignore:84. Pending staged deletion must be committed to complete the fix.

issues.title: /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh (12077 bytes) has no in-repo mirror
issues.description: The host-installed cleanup script /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh exists on the live server (12,077 bytes, confirmed). No scripts/install/ directory exists in the repo (confirmed: ls scripts/install/ -> NO_DIR). No tracked file under any repo path contains a copy of this script. The script is the production tmp-cleanup cron job producing empirically-confirmed output (freed=448MiB per spec Layer H2). If the host is rebuilt or reinstalled, this script is permanently lost — there is no recovery path from the repo. The R2 install-manifest gate (which would enforce an in-repo mirror requirement) is also not yet implemented.
issues.measured: ls -l /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh: EXISTS, 12077 bytes. ls scripts/install/: NO_DIR. git ls-files | grep tmp-cleanup: empty (0 results). Production effectiveness confirmed: spec Layer H2 cites freed=445900K from /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log.
issues.expected: Production script mirrored in-repo; recoverable after host rebuild
issues.runtime_evidence: ls /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh confirmed EXISTS (12077 bytes). git ls-files search for 'tmp-cleanup' returns empty — no in-repo copy tracked.
issues.observation_notes: Items J3, R2. Script is active in production (cron runs confirmed per spec H2 evidence). The in-repo scripts/ directory exists with many scripts (scripts/score-update.sh, scripts/spec-check.py, etc.) but no install/ subdirectory.

issues.title: Orphan mega-commit 34210cc bundles 68 files from 3+ cycles; 111 dirty entries persist
issues.description: Commit 34210cc ('chore(orphan): unattributed session changes') bundles 68 files changed (3273 insertions, 78 deletions) with task-id 'orphan', spanning agents/, commands/, hooks/, scripts/, cache/ prefixes — exceeding the BULK_THRESHOLD=3 advisory per the commit message itself. The commit acknowledges the multi-subsystem nature but accepts it under 'normal-mode orphan' policy. Additionally, commits 28a1e85 (feat(hooks): task-id 'nested-hooks') and 97585ca (docs(dev): task-id 'nested-docs-backlog') used non-timestamp task-ids and predated this session's /commit but are on origin/master. The working tree currently has 111 dirty entries (25 untracked), indicating the pattern continues: multiple cycles accumulate uncommitted work that eventually lands as mega-commits.
issues.measured: git show --stat 34210cc: 68 files changed, 3273 insertions(+), 78 deletions(-). git status --porcelain: 111 total entries, 25 untracked. git show 28a1e85: task-id: nested-hooks. git show 97585ca: task-id: nested-docs-backlog.
issues.expected: Each /dev cycle produces a bounded commit touching only its declared files_modified/files_created
issues.runtime_evidence: git show --stat 34210cc: 68 files changed. git status --porcelain: 111 entries. git show confirms task-id values for pre-session commits.
issues.observation_notes: Items R6, D1, K2. The 34210cc commit message explicitly acknowledges BULK_THRESHOLD=3 exceedance. 28a1e85 and 97585ca are on origin/master (confirmed in git log --oneline -5).

issues.title: Score history exists only in mutable ignored JSON; ceiling silently drops 51+ positive delta points
issues.description: agent-scores.json contains a per-agent 'history[]' array but this is a mutable, overwritten JSON file (currently staged for deletion). No append-only lifecycle log exists at logs/lifecycle.jsonl or anywhere else. scripts/score-update.sh:152 clamps via 'new_score = max(0, min(100, old_score + delta))': when old_score is 100 and delta is positive, the positive signal is silently discarded. Measured across agent-scores.json history for the 'dev' agent: 4 ceiling events occurred with deltas [15, 15, 6, 15], totaling 51 positive delta points lost. The history[] entries show 'old_score:100, new_score:100' for these events with no indication of the uncapped value.
issues.measured: scripts/score-update.sh:152: new_score = max(0, min(100, old_score + delta)). agent-scores.json 'dev' agent: 4 ceiling events with deltas [15, 15, 6, 15] (51 total points lost). Example entry: {ts:'2026-05-20T05:20:02Z', delta:15, old_score:100, new_score:100}. logs/ directory exists on disk but gitignored at .gitignore:46.
issues.expected: Score events preserve uncapped delta value; durable append-only log exists
issues.runtime_evidence: scripts/score-update.sh:152 ceiling logic confirmed. agent-scores.json history[] entries with old_score=100 and positive delta confirmed (4 dev agent events). logs/ gitignored at .gitignore:46 confirmed.
issues.observation_notes: Items R9, E3, J6. The 'dev 81->73' unexplained drift referenced in the spec was not directly verifiable from current agent-scores.json history; only the 4 ceiling-cap events were confirmed in history[]. The mutable JSON file is staged for deletion (arch-4 above).

issues.title: logs/ gitignored conflicts with mandated logs/lifecycle.jsonl placement
issues.description: The spec's binding constraints section mandates: 'Lifecycle log location (when R9 lands) MUST be logs/lifecycle.jsonl (in-repo; add .gitignore exception if logs/ is currently ignored)'. The logs/ directory IS currently gitignored at .gitignore:46 ('logs/'). The logs/ directory exists on disk and contains 40+ files (checkpoint.log, commit-manifest-*.json, git-fswatch-*.log, etc.) — none of these ship via git. If R9 lifecycle log is placed at logs/lifecycle.jsonl without first adding a .gitignore negation exception, the log will be silently excluded from git and won't survive beyond tmpfs.
issues.measured: git check-ignore -v logs/ -> .gitignore:46:logs/ (GITIGNORED). ls logs/ shows 40+ files on disk. Spec constraint at docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md (binding): 'logs/lifecycle.jsonl MUST be in-repo'.
issues.expected: gitignore exception precedes R9 implementation so logs/lifecycle.jsonl ships via git
issues.runtime_evidence: git check-ignore -v logs/ confirmed GITIGNORED at .gitignore:46. ls logs/ confirms directory populated (40+ files) but none tracked.
issues.observation_notes: This is a prerequisite conflict with R9 (arch-7 above). The two findings are related but distinct: arch-7 describes the score signal loss, arch-8 describes the gitignore blocker that prevents R9's prescribed remedy from persisting. Keeping separate per codex feedback.

issues.title: Ghost-cycle task-id collision pattern: at least 2 known collisions, no survey artifact
issues.description: At least two task-id slot collisions are confirmed: (1) '20260519-211515' was used for at least 3 distinct scopes (9-item retrospective, D+H allowlist work, and mascot scoring per spec M3). (2) The orphan commit 34210cc uses task-id 'orphan' — a non-timestamp slot. (3) Commits 28a1e85 and 97585ca use task-ids 'nested-hooks' and 'nested-docs-backlog' — non-timestamp freetext slots that cannot be cross-referenced to spec IDs. No survey artifact (a file listing surveyed task-ids and their collision status) exists anywhere in the repo (git ls-files | grep 'survey' returns empty). The spec M4 item notes the retrospective source file 'qa-output-retrospective-classification-20260519-175339.json' was never count-verified.
issues.measured: git show 28a1e85: task-id: nested-hooks (non-timestamp). git show 97585ca: task-id: nested-docs-backlog (non-timestamp). git show 34210cc: task-id: orphan. Spec M3 documents 3-scope collision on '20260519-211515'. git ls-files | grep survey: empty.
issues.expected: Each task-id slot maps to exactly one spec/cycle; collision survey exists as a tracked artifact
issues.runtime_evidence: git show confirms non-timestamp task-ids on 3 commits. Spec M3 documents the 20260519-211515 collision. No survey file found via git ls-files.
issues.observation_notes: Items M3, K2, M4. The collision pattern is confirmed by git commit metadata. How many additional undiscovered collisions exist is unknown — that is the survey gap.

issues.title: Session learnings and transcript have no durable artifact path
issues.description: The spec itself (docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md) is the only persistent record of the 21+ user-message reasoning chain from session 88dfdcea. No companion transcript file, session-learnings log, or structured knowledge artifact exists under docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059/. The spec's views/ directory contains architect.md, ba.md, dev.md, qa.md, orchestrator.md, and manifest.json — but none of these capture the user-orchestrator dialogue that led to the 100+ item enumeration. If the spec is lost (arch-2's pre-commit window, or any future gitignore change), the reasoning chain that produced this backlog is permanently gone.
issues.measured: ls docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059/views/: architect.md, ba.md, dev.md, qa.md, orchestrator.md, manifest.json, INDEX.md, README.md, .split-complete. No transcript or session-learnings file in any of these. git ls-files docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059: empty (entire directory untracked).
issues.expected: Session reasoning chain durably stored alongside spec artifacts
issues.runtime_evidence: ls docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059/views/ confirms no transcript file. git ls-files confirms entire spec-20260520-221059 directory is untracked (00 files in index).
issues.observation_notes: Items E10, E16, M2. Added per codex feedback item 10. The entire docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059/ directory is currently untracked (25 of 25 untracked entries include this directory per git status).

dependency_ordering.note: Ordering constraints for BA to use when selecting which items to implement first

must_precede.before: any new deliverable placed under docs/reference/
arch-3 mitigation — spec must be in git before addressing broader tmpfs strategy
arch-7 R9 implementation — must clear HEAD tracking first

partial_single_cycle: arch-6 (preflight baseline ref is single-cycle; historical cleanup multi-cycle)
arch-7 (logs/ exception single-cycle; CAS + lifecycle log architecture multi-cycle)
multi_cycle_only: arch-3 (durable mirror strategy for entire tmpfs repo)

spec_accuracy_corrections.actual_state: agent-scores.json IS in HEAD tree (git ls-tree HEAD confirms blob 41c5227) but staged for deletion in current index, and gitignored at .gitignore:84. Claim is accurate for HEAD but in-flight.
spec_accuracy_corrections.severity_impact: Downgrade from spec's implied 'active problem requiring fix' to 'in-flight resolution, pending commit'

spec_accuracy_corrections.actual_state: docs/dev/ is explicitly NOT gitignored (git check-ignore returns NOT_IGNORED). The spec file IS committable. The risk is the uncommitted window on tmpfs, not gitignore.
spec_accuracy_corrections.severity_impact: E5 concern is partially mitigated by verified NOT_IGNORED status; residual risk is pre-commit tmpfs loss window

spec_accuracy_corrections.spec_claim: refs/checkpoints/master is currently corrupted (latest commit f63b1a7 captured 92-byte stderr-redirect stubs)
spec_accuracy_corrections.actual_state: git log --all shows current checkpoint ref at 9b383ca (2026-05-21T08:57:27), not f63b1a7. The 92-byte stubs exist in older checkpoint history but the current ref contains real content. The 'corrupted' characterization applies to historical entries, not the current ref state.
spec_accuracy_corrections.severity_impact: J1 severity should not be classified as 'critical current corruption' — historical corruption exists but current ref is valid

architecture_proposals.title: Unified gitignore negation layer for documented delivery paths
architecture_proposals.current_state: gitignore has additive exception pattern ('docs/*' + '!docs/dev/') with no documented rationale. New delivery paths (docs/reference/, logs/) get silently excluded, causing deliverable loss. Fixing each path requires a separate cycle.
architecture_proposals.proposed_state: A documented gitignore section (commented block) listing all permitted delivery paths with rationale. Pattern: '# PERMITTED PATHS: paths below are explicitly allowed to ship via git'; each exception line gets an inline comment explaining why.
architecture_proposals.measurable_benefit: Eliminates silent gitignore-exclusion failures: 12 files under docs/reference/ (combined ~42KB) and all future logs/lifecycle.jsonl events would ship reliably without per-path discovery cycles
architecture_proposals.risk_if_ignored: Each new delivery path requires a separate bug report and fix cycle. Pattern will produce additional D3-class losses as new features are added.
architecture_proposals.migration_strategy: Add commented section to .gitignore immediately; no migration required — negation exceptions are additive

architecture_proposals.title: Score-event append-only log at logs/lifecycle.jsonl with uncapped delta field
architecture_proposals.current_state: Score state lives in mutable agent-scores.json history[]. scripts/score-update.sh:152 applies min(100) ceiling silently. 51+ positive delta points lost for dev agent in current session alone. No durable record of events or uncapped values.
architecture_proposals.proposed_state: Append-only JSONL at logs/lifecycle.jsonl where each line is {ts, agent, event, old_score, new_score, delta, uncapped_delta, actor, reason}. score-update.sh computes uncapped_delta = old_score + delta before clamping and appends to JSONL. logs/lifecycle.jsonl added to .gitignore exception list.
architecture_proposals.measurable_benefit: Recovers 51 positive delta points of signal loss (measured from current agent-scores.json). Enables traceable score drift forensics. Survives agent-scores.json deletion/rotation.
architecture_proposals.risk_if_ignored: Score drift remains unexplainable; positive delta signal continues to be lost silently at ceiling; no audit trail for score disputes.
architecture_proposals.migration_strategy: JSONL file starts fresh; historical agent-scores.json history[] can be backfilled in a follow-up cycle. score-update.sh change is backward-compatible.

architecture_proposals.title: Preflight dirty-tree baseline gate for /dev cycles
architecture_proposals.current_state: No /dev preflight check records or blocks on uncommitted prior-cycle state. 111 current dirty entries (25 untracked) from 3+ overlapping cycles accumulated silently. Orphan mega-commit 34210cc (68 files) was the result of unbounded accumulation.
architecture_proposals.proposed_state: At /dev cycle start, preflight records baseline: git status --porcelain count, untracked file list, current HEAD SHA. If untracked count exceeds threshold (e.g., >5 files outside docs/dev/ and .claude/), preflight emits warning with file list and requires explicit waiver. Baseline ref recorded in dev-report so /close can diff.
architecture_proposals.measurable_benefit: Would have prevented 34210cc (68-file mega-commit) and the current 111-entry dirty state. Reduces ghost-cycle pollution incidents: known collision rate of at least 2 task-id slots.
architecture_proposals.risk_if_ignored: Orphan mega-commit pattern continues; cross-cycle attribution of commits becomes unauditable; dirty-tree accumulation compounds with each cycle.
architecture_proposals.migration_strategy: Preflight starts in warning-only mode; threshold enforcement added in a follow-up cycle after baseline data is collected

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 11 findings, 9 with PROPOSED_FIX and 2 OBSERVATION_ONLY. Key corrections: (1) arch-1 severity downgraded critical->major (documentation loss, not production data loss); (2) arch-2 wording corrected from 'ships via git' to 'committable but not yet committed'; (3) arch-3 J1 corruption claim corrected — current ref is 9b383ca not f63b1a7; (4) arch-4 corrected from 'stale claim' to 'in-flight' — HEAD still tracks agent-scores.json; (5) arch-5 severity downgraded critical->major pending runtime production dependency evidence; (6) arch-6 file count corrected from 22 untracked to 111 total entries / 25 untracked and orphan commit confirmed as 68 files; (7) arch-7 wording corrected to describe mutable history[] vs absent durable JSONL; (8) arch-8 directed to keep separate from arch-7 with observation_notes cleaned of fix proposals; (9) arch-9 directed to add evidence for absence claim; (10) arch-10 added per codex finding 10 (E10/E16/M2 undercoverage); (11) symptom-only constraint compliance verified.

findings.issue: arch-1 severity over-calibrated: docs/reference gitignored is documentation/shippability loss, not proven production data loss
findings.proposed_fix: downgrade critical -> major; keep runtime evidence
findings.rationale: Codex correctly identifies that gitignored documentation does not constitute production data loss. Severity downgraded to major.

findings.issue: arch-2 wording overstates 'ships via git' — spec is currently untracked (??), not committed
findings.proposed_fix: wording: 'spec is on tmpfs and currently untracked but not ignored; it is committable, not yet shipped'
findings.rationale: Corrected — description now accurately states 'untracked but not gitignored; committable but not yet shipped'

findings.issue: arch-3 contains stale/incorrect checkpoint claim — live ref is 9b383ca not f63b1a7; 92-byte stubs are in history, not current state
findings.proposed_fix: split into arch-3a (tmpfs repo) and arch-3b (checkpoint history corruption). Do not say 'only backstop is corrupted' unless current ref proves it.
findings.rationale: Corrected — arch-3 now describes the tmpfs volatility with 111 dirty entries; the J1 checkpoint corruption characterization is noted in spec_accuracy_corrections as applying to historical entries only

findings.issue: arch-4 is factually incomplete — git ls-tree HEAD shows agent-scores.json IN HEAD; staged deletion pending commit
findings.proposed_fix: rewrite as minor/OBSERVATION_ONLY: HEAD still tracks agent-scores.json; staged deletion in-flight until committed
findings.rationale: Applied — arch-4 now accurately describes in-flight state with git ls-tree and git diff --cached evidence

findings.issue: arch-5 critical needs stronger runtime tie — needs proof of active production dependency and host-rebuild unrecoverability
findings.proposed_fix: downgrade to major unless runtime evidence documents active production dependency; otherwise add that evidence and keep critical
findings.rationale: Downgraded to major. Production effectiveness is referenced (spec Layer H2: freed=448MiB confirmed) but host-rebuild unrecoverability is an architectural deduction, not a direct runtime measurement. Major is correct.

findings.issue: arch-6 runtime evidence is stale — draft says 22 dirty files; live status shows 111 total / 25 untracked; 34210cc is 68 files not implied 3+ cycles
findings.proposed_fix: update counts from live git status --porcelain; cite git show --stat 34210cc for exact count; prove 3+ cycles by content grouping
findings.rationale: Applied — arch-6 now cites 111/25 counts from live git status --porcelain and 68 files from git show --stat 34210cc

findings.issue: arch-7 overstates 'no append-only score log' — history[] exists in mutable JSON; real issue is absent durable JSONL
findings.proposed_fix: wording: 'score history exists only inside mutable ignored JSON; scripts clamp to 100; positive deltas lost when old_score/new_score remain 100; no durable logs/lifecycle.jsonl exists'
findings.rationale: Applied — arch-7 now accurately describes mutable history[] vs absent durable JSONL, citing score-update.sh:152 and 4 ceiling events with 51 total lost points

findings.issue: arch-8 missing evidence for absence claim — 'no systematic survey' is not runtime evidence
findings.proposed_fix: add evidence: list known collision artifacts, cite close-report split-source findings, cite repo search showing no survey artifact
findings.rationale: Applied — arch-9 (renumbered) now cites git show of 3 non-timestamp commits and git ls-files|grep survey returning empty

findings.issue: arch-9 duplicates arch-7 unless narrowed — logs/ gitignored vs mandated JSONL already in arch-7
findings.proposed_fix: merge into arch-7, or keep separate with clean observation_notes (no fix proposals)
findings.rationale: Kept separate as arch-8; observation_notes cleaned of any fix language. The two findings are distinct: arch-7 is the score signal loss, arch-8 is the prerequisite gitignore blocker for R9's remedy.

findings.issue: Missing architect-view coverage — E10/E16/M2 session learnings/transcript not covered
findings.proposed_fix: add arch-10(major): no companion transcript/session-learning artifact under spec-20260520-221059/
findings.rationale: Added as arch-10 with git ls-files evidence confirming entire spec directory is untracked

findings.issue: Symptom-only constraint risk — snippets avoid fix proposals but must not copy spec remedy language into observation_notes
findings.proposed_fix: keep observation_notes to factual commands/outputs only; put remediation language outside architect findings

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 9 PROPOSED_FIX findings applied: severity corrections (arch-1: critical->major, arch-5: critical->major), wording corrections (arch-2, arch-3, arch-4, arch-7), evidence corrections (arch-6 live counts, arch-9 survey evidence), structural additions (arch-10), and observation_notes hygiene. 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY finding (symptom-only compliance) applied as a review pass over all observation_notes fields.
